Patch Set 3:

Arshad, regarding this crash reported by Janitor test I assume it could be fixed by one of changes from fallocate punch patch, particularly in vvp_io_setattr_lock()

Patch Set 3:

To check this you could just rebase this patch on top of fallocate punch one

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

This should probably also get an option to turn on/off the test like migrate and other new tests, in case there is a problem after it lands. 

I see in other comments that there are assertions even with the short duration, but eventually we want it to pass for a longer run.

Patch Set 3:

>Arshad, regarding this crash reported by Janitor test I assume it could be fixed by one of changes from fallocate punch patch, particularly in vvp_io_setattr_lock()

Mike. I get the same crash. Please note that this is done in loop to get the crash. I do not get the crash immediately. The racer runs by default are 300s. The way it is run is shown below and I get crash in some iteration which is random but > 5 (at least what I have seen).

$ MOUNT_2="yes" bash ./lustre/tests/llmount.sh
for i in {1..50} 
do 
 $ bash ./lustre/tests/racer.sh
 $ sleep 1
done

>This should probably also get an option to turn on/off the test like migrate and other new tests, in case there is a problem after it lands. 

Andreas, I will add these.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Mike Thanks! - I think 'racer' patch should be rebased on top of yours. I am pulling in 'racer' patch on top of yours... and giving it a go. I will update soon.

Patch Set 4:

Mike, with the below patch series order... racer tests worked over hour and a half. Thanks!!

# git log --format=oneline -3
c22cb145203389e8c0b7fa3fb9c34eb75de7238a LU-14287 tests: Add 'fallocate' to racer
5196072fb5b6053962d0ac629e3934ca1932d1eb LU-14433 llite: do fallocate() size checks under lock
4b95c059b4b7e75c418d084fc74541db3ac33db1 LU-14160 fallocate: Add punch mode to fallocate
#

It would be good to rebase this on top of Mike's fallocate fix patch 41668 to ensure that fixes the problem. 

However, I'll leave this patch running to see at least if the custom run finishes, but it would be good to update and rebase this patch once that is finished to avoid running 50h of irrelevant tests.

Patch Set 15:

Possibly file_fallocate.sh needs to be added to Lustre/tests/(racer?)/Makefile.am and/or .spec/.deb files so it is included into the RPM?

The fact that testing with fallocate triggers segfaults is the whole point of racer.  Testing in isolation doesn't really tell you much, since there are no other operations on the file. But there must be a bug in fallocate in conjunction with some other operation. If it is easily repeated, this is much more easily debugged on your local system instead of via autotest, unless the stack traces of the already-run tests make it clear where the problem is.

Patch Set 16:

Andreas, the below segfault seen when running racer suite is caused by fallocate writing over the same files which "file_exec" created. Racer, we create the same set of filenames. IMHO, while this may be ok for chmod etc... for fallocate this is failing as it overwrites the contents, which may be a valid ELF binary and not a data/flat file. I am wondering if fallocate.sh is a valid candidate for racer as it overwrites the file. ?

--snip--
centos79 kernel: 7[32212]: segfault at 8 ip 00007ffb0ef467e8 sp 00007ffc2fec78f0 error 4 in ld-2.17.so[7ffb0ef3b000+22000]
centos79 kernel: 8[1256]: segfault at e ip 0000000000405c18 sp 00007fff1d804628 error 6 in 8[400000+6000]
centos79kernel: 4[4260]: segfault at 8 ip 00007f36650f17e8 sp 00007ffedfc38b10 error 4 in ld-2.17.so[7f36650e6000+22000]
--snip--

This could be reproduced as below...

 $ cp -a /usr/bin/sleep ./new_sleep # This is done by racer/file_exec.sh
 $ ./new_sleep .2
 $ fallocate -p -o 4500 -l 100 ./new_sleep # Ask fallocate to "smash" sleep binary (file_fallocate.sh) part of racer run
 $ ./new_sleep .2
 Segmentation fault
 
 $ dmesg
 ...
 centos79 kernel: new_sleep[14300]: segfault at 1c ip 00000000004011b0 sp 00007ffe6a68c160 error 6 in new_sleep[400000+6000]

Patch Set 16:

Yes, it is possible that fallocate will corrupt the executable. That shouldn't happen while the executable is running, but may happen beforehand. The same already happens with write and truncate, and there isn't really anything that can be done about it. 

I misunderstood your previous comment, thinking that there were kernel oops/crashes happening. Segfault of the userspace binary is a known problem.

Patch Set 16:

>Segfault of the userspace binary is a known problem.

Understood. Thanks for the clarification.
